stories: Good evening Sean, it's Saturday evening, the sixteenth of May 2026
Hope you've had a relaxing weekend so far
Fun fact to kick things off: today is the 319th birthday of Carl Linnaeus, the Swedish botanist who invented binomial nomenclature, the system we still use to name every living thing on Earth
Every species name traces back to a man born on this day in 1707
Your calendar looks clear for Sunday, so tomorrow is yours to shape
Singapore Airlines has posted record revenue of 20
5 billion Singapore dollars for its financial year to March, with operating profit up 39%
Net profit fell 57% due to Air India losses, but management calls it the price of a long game in a huge market
Good evening Sean, it's Saturday evening, the sixteenth of May 2026
Hope you've had a relaxing weekend so far
Fun fact to kick things off: today is the 319th birthday of Carl Linnaeus, the Swedish botanist who invented binomial nomenclature, the system we still use to name every living thing on Earth
Every species name traces back to a man born on this day in 1707
Your calendar looks clear for Sunday, so tomorrow is yours to shape
Singapore Airlines has posted record revenue of 20
5 billion Singapore dollars for its financial year to March, with operating profit up 39%
Net profit fell 57% due to Air India losses, but management calls it the price of a long game in a huge market
Good morning Sean, it's Friday, the fifteenth of May 2026
Fun fact: May is the only month in the English language that cannot be abbreviated
Every other month shortens neatly - May just refuses
In Singapore: the city's police are trialling jetpacks and weaponised drones to protect its waterways - more sci-fi than patrol right now, but a sign of where maritime security is heading
A new Citi report shows 88 percent of Singaporeans expect inflation to rise this year, despite actual inflation sitting at just 1
The new cruise terminal opens July fifteenth with VIP facilities and automated baggage handling
And despite a regional aviation crisis that hit three thousand flights across Asia yesterday, Changi held firm with 104 delays and zero cancellations
Now the big global story: the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing has concluded, and Xi Jinping has offered to broker a peace deal with Iran
Good morning Sean, it's Friday, the fifteenth of May 2026
Fun fact: May is the only month in the English language that cannot be abbreviated
Every other month shortens neatly - May just refuses
In Singapore: the city's police are trialling jetpacks and weaponised drones to protect its waterways - more sci-fi than patrol right now, but a sign of where maritime security is heading
A new Citi report shows 88 percent of Singaporeans expect inflation to rise this year, despite actual inflation sitting at just 1
3 percent - a gap that shapes consumer behaviour
The new cruise terminal opens July fifteenth with VIP facilities and automated baggage handling
And despite a regional aviation crisis that hit three thousand flights across Asia yesterday, Changi held firm with 104 delays and zero cancellations
Good evening Sean, it's Thursday evening, the fourteenth of May 2026
Your fun fact: on this day in 1948, exactly 78 years ago, David Ben-Gurion declared the establishment of the State of Israel
Within eleven minutes, President Truman recognised the new nation, making the US the first country to do so
Your calendar looks clear for tomorrow, so Friday is yours to shape
Singapore's airports were caught up in a major Asian aviation disruption today
Over two thousand eight hundred flights were delayed and more than four hundred cancelled across the region, with Changi among those affected
Worth bearing in mind if you or the team are travelling tomorrow
The big story globally is the Trump-Xi summit playing out live in Beijing
